_meta.usage: Each object's "tag" is an allowed value for catalog JSON TAGS / TAG (see .design/SCHEMA.md). Append entries as the vocabulary grows. Files under json/tags/ are not loaded as catalog rows.

tags.description: 1000 Genomes Project data, phases, or reference panels.
LLM or agent-style tools for genetics workflows (AI section).
Bayesian inference for PRS, fine-mapping, or related models.
Large population cohort or biobank resource (default Tools-adjacent badge).
Methods designed for very large cohorts (e.g. UKB-scale computation).
Copy-number variation (sequence- or read-based).
Colocalization of GWAS with QTL or other molecular traits.
Cross-population or trans-ethnic comparison, transfer, or modeling.
Neural or deep models for prediction, sequence, or imaging.
Expression quantitative trait loci (bulk or single-cell).
Methylation, chromatin, or other epigenetic layers.
Gene or transcript expression resources (e.g. bulk tissue panels).
Related samples, pedigree structure, or family GWAS.
Credible sets, causal variant prioritization, or regional refinement.
Large sequence or multimodal models for DNA, regulatory, or protein context (AI catalog topic).
Variant consequence, scores, or functional priors for association.
Integrated functional data (eQTL, chromatin, expression context).
Gene- or region-level association (burden, SKAT, meta-gene tests).
Prioritizing causal genes at GWAS loci.
Shared genetic architecture between traits (e.g. LDSC-related).
Genotype-Tissue Expression project or GTEx-derived resources.
Genome-wide association study methods or results.
Gene–environment interaction analyses.
HapMap-era panels or legacy reference haplotypes.
SNP-chip or partitioned heritability estimation.
HLA typing, imputation, or MHC-focused association.
Brain or other imaging-derived GWAS / endophenotypes.
Genotype imputation servers, panels, or phasing workflows.
Publication venue entry (default Journals section badge).
Repeated measures, trajectories, or within-subject variability.
MR, causal inference from germline instruments.
Combining studies or summary statistics across cohorts.
Metabolite or metabolic trait GWAS / resources.
Multiple genetic ancestries or trans-ancestry scope in one resource.
Joint layers (e.g. genomics + proteomics + metabolomics).
Multiple traits or phenotypes; multi-trait fine-mapping or meta-methods.
Ordered categorical or graded outcomes.
Pangenome graphs, graph genomes, or pangenome-aware genotyping.
Gene-set, pathway, or enrichment-style follow-up.
Phenotype coding, linkage, or deep phenotyping in a cohort.
Multiple traits per variant or shared signal modeling.
Selection, demography, admixture, genealogy, or related population-level inference.
Secure or federated association without sharing individual genotypes.
Multi-phase flagship program (default Projects section badge).
Polygenic risk or liability scores; PGS calibration and transfer.
Protein-level GWAS, assays, or proteomic sumstats.
Rare or low-frequency variant association (e.g. RVAS, gene tests).
Reference panel, genome build, or baseline population resource.
Single-cell chromatin accessibility integration with genetics.
Single-cell RNA integration with GWAS or QTL context.
Power, null, or method benchmarking simulators.
Single-cell methods or atlases (default Single_Cell section badge).
Spatial transcriptomics or imaging-based spatial assays.
Splicing QTL or splice-related molecular QTL.
SVs, CNVs at genome scale, or graph-based SV typing.
Summary-statistic input/output; default Sumstats section badge.
Survival, censored, or time-to-event phenotypes.
Software or package (default Tools section badge).
RNA-seq, expression GWAS, or transcript-level sumstats.
Transcriptome-wide association; gene expression–mediated association.
UK Biobank data, phenotypes, or UKB-focused methods.
Plots, browsers, or GWAS/variant visualization tools.
Whole-exome sequencing data or exome-first analyses.
Whole-genome sequencing data or WGS-first analyses.
